heading: Waiver of parental consent
label: 3206 Waiver of parental consent
notes: Change of Name“Federal magistrate judge” substituted for “Federal magistrate” in subsec. (d) pursuant to , set out as a note under , Judiciary and Judicial Procedure.section 321 of Pub. L. 101–650section 631 of Title 28

children.heading: Examinations and interviews
children.label: a Examinations and interviews
children.text: Photographs, x-rays, medical examinations, psychological examinations, and interviews of an Indian child alleged to have been subject to abuse in Indian country shall be allowed without parental consent if local child protective services or local law enforcement officials have reason to believe the child has been subject to abuse.

children.heading: Interviews by law enforcement and child protective services officials
children.label: b Interviews by law enforcement and child protective services officials
children.text: In any case in which officials of the local law enforcement agency or local child protective services agency have reason to believe that an Indian child has been subject to abuse in Indian country, the officials of those agencies shall be allowed to interview the child without first obtaining the consent of the parent, guardian, or legal custodian.

children.heading: Protection of child
children.label: c Protection of child
children.text: Examinations and interviews of a child who may have been the subject of abuse shall be conducted under such circumstances and with such safeguards as are designed to minimize additional trauma to the child and, where time permits, shall be conducted with the advise, or under the guidance, of a local multidisciplinary team established pursuant to or, in the absence of a local team, a multidisciplinary team established pursuant to .1section 3210 of this titlesection 3209 of this titleSo in original. Probably should be “advice”.1

children.heading: Court orders
children.label: d Court orders
children.text: Upon a finding of reasonable suspicion that an Indian child has been the subject of abuse in Indian country, a Federal magistrate judge or United States District Court may issue an order enforcing any provision of this section.
